prompt: Show a copper plate half-heated by an alcohol lamp.

vqa_checklist.question: There is an alcohol lamp in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. Irrelevant to the current world knowledge question, confirms the existence of the basic experimental heating equipment entity. PASS when an alcohol lamp appears in the image; FAIL when it does not appear.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a metal plate in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. Irrelevant to the current world knowledge question, confirms the existence of the basic entity of the heated substance. PASS when a metal plate or a long, thin metal strip appears in the image; FAIL when it does not appear.

vqa_checklist.question: The flame of the alcohol lamp is in contact with and is heating half of the metal plate.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. Confirms the spatial and physical interaction between the heating device and the heated object. PASS when the flame of the alcohol lamp is in direct contact with or wrapping around half of the metal plate being heated; FAIL if it is not in contact, is too far away, or if the flame completely wraps around the entire metal plate.

vqa_checklist.question: The unheated half of the metal plate appears purplish-red, copper-red, or bright red.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical knowledge testing (physical properties of metallic copper). Metallic copper appears bright red, purplish-red, or copper-red at room temperature. PASS when the unheated part of the metal plate in the image presents such metallic colors; FAIL if it appears silvery-white, completely black, or in other colors.

vqa_checklist.question: The surface of the heated half of the metal plate located in the flame appears black.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical reaction phenomenon testing (reaction of copper with oxygen). When copper is heated in the air, it reacts with oxygen to generate black copper oxide (2Cu + O2 = 2CuO). PASS when the surface of the heated part (the part of the metal plate close to or inside the flame) in the image presents an obvious black color; FAIL if it still maintains its original red color, turns grayish-white, or produces intense burning and sparking phenomena.

vqa_checklist.question: There are experimental tools (such as tweezers or crucible tongs) used to hold the metal plate in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Experimental safety common sense. Not explicitly requested. If there is no holding tool in the image (e.g., the metal plate is supported by a stand), skip the evaluation and assign a silent NA; if the model actively generates a tool, PASS when the tool (such as tweezers or crucible tongs) correctly holds the unheated end of the metal plate and stays away from the flame; FAIL when a human hand directly holds the metal plate, or the tool is holding the area being burned by the flame.

vqa_checklist.question: There are additional text annotations in the image explaining the chemical reaction, equation, or substance names.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional knowledge annotation task. Not explicitly requested. If there is no additional text in the image, assign a silent NA. If the model actively generates text, PASS when the text content is related to the oxidation reaction of copper (e.g., containing words like 'Cu', 'CuO', 'O2', 'Oxidation') and is spelled correctly; FAIL when the text has spelling errors or the content is unrelated to this chemical reaction.

vqa_checklist.question: The additional text annotations in the image correctly correspond to the state areas of the metal plate before and after the reaction.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional knowledge annotation task. Not explicitly requested. If there is no additional text in the image, assign a silent NA. If the model actively generates text, PASS if it correctly points the 'Cu' or copper annotation to the unheated red part, and points the 'CuO' or copper oxide annotation to the blackened part (can be via connecting lines, arrows, or close-proximity layout); FAIL if it points in reverse or if the correspondence cannot be identified.

cot_prompt: A small wide-bottomed glass bottle containing a transparent liquid, with a cotton wick at its mouth burning a flame that is blue on the inside and yellow on the outside. Directly above, the flame contacts and envelops half of a thin rectangular metal sheet. The half of the metal sheet not contacting the flame presents a reddish-brown color with a metallic luster, while the surface of the half being roasted by the flame presents a dull, matte pure black.

additional_vqa_checklist.question: The metal plate in the image is a single, physically continuous piece of board.
additional_vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Semantic understanding and common sense. The prompt requires showing a copper plate "half-heated", which means heating a local area of the same intact metal plate. PASS if the metal plate in the image appears as a single piece of board with continuous surface texture; FAIL if there is a straight gap in the middle of the metal plate, looking as if two independent metal blocks of different colors or states are forcibly pieced together, creating a physical fracture.

additional_vqa_checklist.question: The flame of the heating device in the image has a layered structure, and the base or inner layer of the flame appears bright blue.
additional_vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit physical knowledge (gas combustion phenomenon). The model spontaneously generates a gas torch/blowtorch as the heating source; when such a gas burner burns fully, the base or inner flame should appear bright blue, and the outer flame appears yellow or light blue. PASS if the base or inner layer of the flame emitted in the image appears blue; FAIL if the flame is completely red or yellow, which does not match the characteristics of a high-temperature gas flame.

additional_vqa_checklist.question: The heated area of the metal plate directly touched by the flame exhibits a bright orange-red or yellowish-white high-temperature glowing state.
additional_vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit physical knowledge (thermal radiation/incandescence phenomenon). The blowtorch flame spontaneously generated by the model has an extremely high temperature, and the local metal area directly burned will produce strong thermal radiation due to the high temperature, appearing bright orange-red or yellowish-white (incandescent state). PASS if the metal area in direct contact with the flame in the image shows obvious high-temperature glowing and incandescent visual performance; FAIL if the area in contact with the extremely high-temperature flame is completely dull and fails to show the thermal radiation glowing phenomenon.

additional_vqa_checklist.question: The boundary between the heated blackened area and the unheated copper-red area on the metal plate shows a gradient interference color band containing purple, blue, or yellow.
additional_vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical knowledge (metal thermal oxidation interference colors). When copper is heated in the air, the varying thicknesses of the oxide film generated on the surface will produce thin-film interference. Between the completely blackened copper oxide and the unoxidized copper-red area, transitional temper colors (interference colors) such as purple, blue, and golden yellow usually appear. PASS if gradient interference colors such as purple or blue exist at the boundary between the oxidized blackened area and the unheated area in the image; FAIL if there is only an abrupt black-red boundary without any color transition, or if green (patina) representing slow oxidation at room temperature appears.
